executive_verdict.primary_failure_mode: The live marketing owner loop temporarily lost certifiability because its runner bundle lagged newer momentum/audit evidence, creating a short-lived self-certification gap until the full bundle and independent signoff were refreshed.
executive_verdict.most_urgent_fix: Keep the marketing full-contract path coherence-gated so runner evidence is refreshed whenever newer momentum/audit artifacts would otherwise outrun it.

audit_metadata.scheduler_inspection_method: direct openclaw cron list --json plus jobs.json comparison

owns: bounded timeout reruns and timeout tuning
artifact contract checks for code-backed shared-market reuse
syncing research artifacts into the git repo

findings.title: Marketing full-contract certifiability drifted when newer audit/watchdog evidence outran the runner bundle
findings.mechanism: marketing_loop_checker.py correctly failed closed because marketing_loop_runner_latest.json was older than newer marketing_momentum_watchdog.json and marketing_workflow_audit_latest.json evidence.
findings.root_cause: The live marketing contract depends on runner/audit/momentum coherence, and that coherence temporarily lapsed before the next bundle refresh.
findings.recommended_fix: Refresh the full marketing runner bundle immediately when coherence breaks, then regenerate fresh independent verification before re-certifying the loop.

findings.title: Marketing learning is now runtime-backed, but measurable outcome movement is still absent
findings.mechanism: The refreshed marketing audit still reports flat Codeberg adoption and flags repetitive outreach/execution-ceiling patterns under a measurement-pending repair window.
findings.root_cause: The loop has improved its self-diagnosis and certifiability faster than it has improved real distribution results.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep replacing tactics and system design until Codeberg adoption moves; do not let measurement-pending status become a resting state.

findings.title: Retired topology is still present in jobs.json but remains explicitly disabled and non-authoritative
findings.mechanism: Three legacy jobs remain in the persisted scheduler file for audit history, while the live enabled runtime exposed by openclaw cron list --json is 20 jobs with no stray user crontab entries.
findings.root_cause: Historical retention is intentional and explicitly non-live rather than silently active.
findings.recommended_fix: Keep legacy jobs disabled, clearly described as legacy, and out of the live ownership path.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Preserve marketing loop coherence between runner, audit, momentum, and independent verification artifacts
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: This was the only live certifiability gap found in the current run.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The marketing stack fails closed on drift and regains independent certification immediately after repair.

ordered_fix_plan.goal: Convert marketing self-improvement into measurable Codeberg adoption movement
ordered_fix_plan.why_now: The loop is structurally healthier than before, but outcomes are still flat.
ordered_fix_plan.expected_effect: The system stops rewarding activity-only marketing work and forces stronger tactic or architecture replacement.

repairs_applied_this_run.change: Regenerated the full marketing runner bundle after the checker detected coherence drift against newer momentum/audit evidence.
Ran fresh independent verification after the marketing runner refresh so the repaired marketing path is not self-certified.

independent_verification.summary: Live loop ownership remains coherent, the marketing certifiability gap was repaired and independently re-signed, shared market-intelligence reuse is still machine-verifiable, and health monitoring reports no open system issues.

highest_risk_unresolved_issue.title: Marketing outcomes remain flat despite better loop discipline
highest_risk_unresolved_issue.why: The loop now certifies correctly and promotes findings into runtime behavior, but Codeberg adoption and broader distribution results have still not moved in the current window.
